name: Mediterranean Sea Basin
climate: Mediterranean: hot dry summers, mild wet winters; semi-arid in North Africa; temperate in northern littoral

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: Northern Mediterranean states are EU members; EU migration and trade policy shapes the basin

neighbors.name: Black Sea Region
neighbors.relevance: Connected via Turkish Straits; grain and energy flows transit between both basins

neighbors.name: Suez Canal
neighbors.relevance: Eastern gateway linking Mediterranean to Red Sea and Indo-Pacific shipping

neighbors.name: Levant
neighbors.relevance: Eastern Mediterranean states; East Med gas discoveries and regional instability

neighbors.name: Sahel
neighbors.relevance: Sub-Saharan migration routes terminate at North African Mediterranean coasts

neighbors.name: Red Sea
neighbors.relevance: Suez-Red Sea corridor is the primary Europe-Asia shipping lane entering the Mediterranean

links.description: Spain, France, Italy, Greece, Croatia, Slovenia, Malta, Cyprus are EU members on the Mediterranean
Turkish Straits connect Mediterranean to Black Sea; critical for grain and energy transit
Suez Canal handles 12-15% of global trade entering the Mediterranean from the east
Eastern Mediterranean gas fields (Leviathan, Zohr) involve Cyprus, Israel, Egypt, Lebanon
Ukrainian and Russian grain and energy exports flow through Turkish Straits into the Med
North African OPEC members (Algeria, Libya) supply oil and gas to southern European markets
North African AU members are key trade and migration partners for EU Mediterranean states
